input.background: Legalized gambling is available on several riverboat casinos operated by a city in Mississippi. The mayor of the city wants to know the correlation between the number of casino employees and the yearly crime rate. The records for the past 10 years are examined, and the results listed in Table 11.3 are obtained.
input.data:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Table 11.3 & \multicolumn{2}{|l|}{Data on Casino Employees and Crime Rate, Example 11.4} \\
\hline Year & Number of Casino Employees, x (thousands) & Crime Rate, $y$ (number of crimes per 1,000 population) \\
\hline 2003 & 15 & 1.35 \\
\hline 2004 & 18 & 1.63 \\
\hline 2005 & 24 & 2.33 \\
\hline 2006 & 22 & 2.41 \\
\hline 2007 & 25 & 2.63 \\
\hline 2008 & 29 & 2.93 \\
\hline 2009 & 30 & 3.41 \\
\hline 2010 & 32 & 3.26 \\
\hline 2011 & 35 & 3.63 \\
\hline 2012 & 38 & -4.15 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: Calculate the coefficient of correlation $r$ for the data.

output.answer: Rather than use the computing formula given in the definition, we resort to using a statistical software package. The data of Table 11.3 were entered into a computer, and Minitab was used to compute $r$. The Minitab printout is shown in Figure 11.17. The coefficient of correlation, highlighted at the top on the printout, is $r=.987$.
